title: Tell me about yourself — walk me through your background and resume.

transcript.text: Tell me about yourself — walk me through your background and resume.

blocks.md:
## "Tell me about yourself" — assuming a strong tech-company hiring-manager round (no company named, so I'll keep it framework-neutral)
Career arc, not STAR. Present → 2–3 highest-impact projects → why I'm excited. ~150 words spoken.

🎙️ **Say-it script:**

> 💬 "So I'm a backend engineer, about three and a half years in, mostly distributed systems and high-throughput data work.
>
> Right now I'm at Walmart, on the Data Ventures team. I build the supplier-facing data APIs — so companies like Pepsi and Coca-Cola query their Walmart sales and inventory data. The thing I'm proudest of there is a shared audit-logging library. Three teams were duplicating the same code, and we were paying around fifty grand a month for a logging tool we were decommissioning. I built it as a one-dependency Spring Boot starter — handles about two million events a day, and it dropped that cost to roughly five hundred a month.
>
> Before Walmart I was at a startup called Good Creator Co. Tiny team — I was the sole architect on six production services. The big one was moving our analytics off Postgres onto ClickHouse. Postgres was choking at ten million writes a day, latency had crept from five milliseconds to five hundred. I put a buffered queue in front and batched the writes — got analytics queries from thirty seconds down to twelve, and cut infra cost about thirty percent.
>
> And I started out at PayU in loan disbursal — first real job, first time my code moved actual customer money. I got the partner-API failure rate from four-point-six percent down to under half a percent.
>
> What I want next is that kind of platform work but at a bigger scale — that's why I'm here."

💬 **15-second version:**

> 💬 "Backend engineer, three and a half years — Walmart now on supplier data APIs, before that sole architect of six services at a startup, started in fintech at PayU. Strength is high-throughput systems and zero-downtime migrations. Looking to do platform work at a bigger scale."

---

**A few delivery notes:**

- **Keep it to ~60 seconds.** This is an opener, not a deep dive. Land the arc and stop — let them pull the thread they care about.
- **End on forward-looking energy.** "Platform work at a bigger scale" invites them to connect it to the role.
- **Have the hooks ready.** You just name-dropped three concrete things — the audit library, ClickHouse, the PayU reliability fix. Expect the next question to be "tell me more about one of those."

**Likely follow-ups:**
- "Why are you leaving Walmart?" → Positive only: bigger platform/infra scope at higher scale. Never knock Walmart.
- "Walk me deeper through the ClickHouse migration." → Go to the full hero story (Postgres choking → RabbitMQ buffered sinker → MergeTree → 2-week dual-write).
- "What's your strongest language?" → Java (Spring Boot at Walmart/PayU), but Go and Python from the startup — pick based on their stack.
- "Six services solo — how?" → Standardize patterns, refuse ops complexity, push work onto managed services.

**What NOT to say:**
- Don't list every project — three landmarks max, or you'll ramble past a minute.
- Don't say "GCC" out loud — say "Good Creator Co."
- Don't badmouth PayU or Walmart, even lightly.

Want me to tailor this to a specific company (Amazon LP framing, Google, DigitalOcean's 7 values) or drill into one of these stories?
